revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:間人大蓋]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}}}
'''Hashihito no Ōfuta''' (間人大蓋, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[:en:Japanese nobility|nobleman]] and military figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|qid=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}} title was {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|qid=Q2735611|lt=Muraji|1=Muraji}}. He held the Rank of {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinchū|qq=}} under the [[Twenty-six Grades of Cap Rank]] system.
== Lineage == The Hashihito clan (間人連氏) is mentioned in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} under two lineages: the "Sakyō Royal Lineage" as descendants of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}'s son, Hohoya Wake no Mikoto, and the "Sakyō Kami Lineage" as descendants of the fifth-generation grandson of Kamimune no Mikoto, Tamakushi Hiko no Mikoto. Members of the Hashihito clan included figures like {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Shiofuta|ja|間人連塩蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Shiofuta|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mentioned in Emperor Suiko's 18th year (610), and {{ill|Hashihito no Muraji Miumaya|ja|間人連御厩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihito no Muraji Miumaya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mentioned in Emperor Saimei's 3rd year (657). Both individuals were involved in diplomatic relations with {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Volume 22 and Volume 26.</ref>
== Records == According to ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' Volume 27, during Emperor Tenji's 2nd year (663), Ōfuta served as a general in the campaign against Silla: 1. Front Army Generals: {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野君稚子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55406948}} and Hashihito no Muraji Ōfuta 2. Middle Army Generals: {{ill|Kose no Kamusaki no Osamu|ja|巨勢神前訳語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Kamusaki no Osamu|1=Kose no Kamusaki no Omi Osamu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55407023}} and {{ill|Miwa no Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Nemaro|1=Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55407015}} 3. Rear Army Generals: {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|en|Abe no Hirafu|qid=Q479775|lt=Abe no Hirafu|1=Abe no Hirafu}} and {{ill|Ōyake no Kamatuka|ja|大宅鎌柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Kamatuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} The campaign mobilized 27,000 troops.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> Ōfuta’s front army successfully captured two Silla fortresses, Sabikinue, in June.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 6th Month entry.</ref>
The campaign aimed to restore [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]], which had fallen to the Tang-Silla alliance in 660, as a vassal state of Yamato. However, the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|en|Battle of Baekgang|qid=Q608727|lt=Battle of Baekgang|1=Battle of Baekgang}} in August resulted in a decisive defeat for the Yamato-Baekje forces, ending Baekje's restoration efforts.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref>
== Later Life == In Emperor Tenmu's 4th year (675), Ōfuta participated in religious ceremonies. He was assigned to oversee the worship of the {{ill|Ōimi no Kami|ja|大忌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōimi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at Kawawa in Hirose (modern-day {{ill|Hirose Taisha|en|Hirose Taisha|qid=Q705281|lt=Hirose Shrine|1=Hirose Taisha}} in {{ill|Kawai, Nara|en|Kawai, Nara|qid=Q1203880|lt=Kōryō|1=Kawai, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture). His Rank at the time was {{ill|Shōkinchū|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkinchū|qq=}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 4th Year, 4th Month, 10th Day entry.</ref>
According to ''Nihon Shoki'' Volume 29, during the establishment of the {{ill|Eight-Class Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight-Class Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} in Emperor Tenmu's 13th year (684), the Hashihito clan was elevated to the {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|qid=Q11455653|lt=Sukune|1=Sukune}} Rank.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenmu, 13th Year, 12th Month, 2nd Day entry.</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes IV and V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kawabe no Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Momoe|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55406963}} - A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Azumi no Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi no Hirafu|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10946881}} - A general who perished at Baekgang. * {{ill|Saihi Pinang|ja|狭井檳榔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saihi Pinang|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55406942}} - Another general in the battle. * {{ill|Rokuhara no Omi|ja|廬原臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuhara no Omi|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55407030}} - A general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Suruga Castle|ja|周留城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Castle|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11416926}} * {{ill|Liu Rengui|en|Liu Rengui|qid=Q701534|lt=Liu Ren Gui|1=Liu Rengui}} * {{ill|Munmu of Silla|en|Munmu of Silla|qid=Q494532|lt=King Munmu|1=Munmu of Silla}} * {{ill|Kim Yu-sin|en|Kim Yu-sin|qid=Q494501|lt=Kim Yu-sin|1=Kim Yu-sin}}
{{translated page|ja|間人大蓋|version=88849963|comment=Imported full ja history}}
